findings.description: The consensus report contract requires `primary_finding_ref` in `<vendor>-<finding_id>` form, but the plan artifacts do not define who constructs that vendor-prefixed identifier or how it maps back to raw vendor findings. That leaves consensus entries ambiguously linked to their source reviews and weakens the review gate's traceability.
findings.resolution: Define the mapping rule explicitly in the spec/design, or change the contract to carry separate `vendor` and `finding_id` fields instead of an implicit composite string.

findings.description: The proposal promises a `reviews/review-manifest.json` artifact capturing vendor execution, timing, and quorum status, but the tasks and work packages never assign ownership for creating or validating that file. As planned, one of the declared review outputs will not be produced.
findings.resolution: Add an explicit requirement, task, and work-package responsibility for writing `reviews/review-manifest.json`, and include validation that it is emitted with the required metadata.

findings.description: The design's vendor adapter examples interpolate the full review prompt directly into CLI command strings. That pattern encourages shell-based subprocess execution and creates command-injection and argument-handling risks when prompts contain shell metacharacters or become large.
findings.resolution: Require adapters to invoke vendor CLIs with argument lists instead of shell command strings, pass prompts via stdin or temporary files, and document validation and timeout handling at the subprocess boundary.
